verses.2: “Can a strong man be of use to God, Or a wise one be useful to himself?
verses.3: “Is it any pleasure to the Almighty if you are righteous, Or gain if you make your ways blameless?
verses.4: “Is it because of your reverence that He punishes you, That He enters into judgment against you?
verses.5: “Is your wickedness not abundant, And is there no end to your guilty deeds?
verses.6: “For you have seized pledges from your brothers without cause, And stripped people naked.
verses.7: “You have given the weary no water to drink, And you have withheld bread from the hungry.
verses.8: “But the earth belongs to the powerful man, And the one who is honorable dwells on it.
verses.9: “You have sent widows away empty, And the strength of orphans has been crushed.
verses.10: “Therefore traps surround you, And sudden dread terrifies you,
verses.11: Or darkness, so that you cannot see, And a flood of water covers you.
verses.12: “Is God not in the height of heaven? Look also at the highest stars, how high they are!
verses.13: “But you say, ‘What does God know? Can He judge through the thick darkness?
verses.14: ‘Clouds are a hiding place for Him, so that He cannot see; And He walks on the vault of heaven.’
verses.15: “Will you keep to the ancient path Which wicked people have walked,
verses.16: Who were snatched away before their time, Whose foundations were washed away by a river?
verses.17: “They said to God, ‘Go away from us!’ And ‘What can the Almighty do to them?’
verses.18: “Yet He filled their houses with good things; But the advice of the wicked is far from me.
verses.19: “The righteous see and are glad, And the innocent mock them, saying,
verses.20: ‘Truly our enemies are eliminated, And fire has consumed their abundance.’
verses.21: “Be reconciled with Him, and be at peace; Thereby good will come to you.
verses.22: “Please receive instruction from His mouth, And put His words in your heart.
verses.23: “If you return to the Almighty, you will be restored; If you remove injustice far from your tent,
verses.24: And put your gold in the dust, And the gold of Ophir among the stones of the brooks,
verses.25: Then the Almighty will be your gold And abundant silver to you.
verses.26: “For then you will take pleasure in the Almighty And lift up your face to God.
verses.27: “You will pray to Him, and He will hear you; And you will pay your vows.
verses.28: “You will also decide something, and it will be established for you; And light will shine on your ways.
verses.29: “When they have brought you low, you will speak with confidence, And He will save the humble person.
verses.30: “He will rescue one who is not innocent, And he will be rescued due to the cleanness of your hands.”
